title: pinus monophylla subsp. fallax
intro: Pinus monophylla subsp. fallax, also known as the Great Basin bristlecone pine variant, is a subspecies of Pinus monophylla, a small shrub-like evergreen tree native to the Great Basin region of North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pinus monophylla subsp. fallax include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function. Pinus monophylla subsp. fallax is often prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
